zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Fitzwilliam於1302年出生在英國越可爾艾姆利時，她的父親William FitzWilliam49歲，母親Isabel Maud Deincourt27歲。她和Sir Henry de Pierrepont至少有2個兒子。她去世於1348年之後在她的家鄉。

nameMeanings.name: Fitzwilliam
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish (of Norman origin): from Anglo-Norman French fiz ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name William .

variants.name: Fitz
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Fitzwilliam was born in 1302, in Emley, Yorkshire, England, her father, William FitzWilliam, was 49 and her mother, Isabel Maud Deincourt, was 27. She had at least 2 sons with Sir Henry de Pierrepont. She died after 1348, in her hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Fitzwilliam est née en 1302, à Emley, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, William FitzWilliam, avait 49 ans et sa mère, Isabel Maud Deincourt, avait 27 ans. Elle a eu au moins 2 fils avec Sir Henry de Pierrepont. Elle est décédée après 1348, dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Fitzwilliam 1302 in Emley, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William FitzWilliam 49 und ihre Mutter Isabel Maud Deincourt 27. Sie hatte mit Sir Henry de Pierrepont mindestens 2 Söhne. Sie starb nach 1348 in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Fitzwilliam è nata il nel 1302 nel Emley, Yorkshire, England, sua padre, William FitzWilliam, aveva 49 anni e sua madre, Isabel Maud Deincourt, aveva 27 anni. Lei avuto almeno 2 figli con Sir Henry de Pierrepont. Morì dopo il 1348, nel sua cittadina.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Fitzwilliamが1302年にイギリスヨークシュアイアエムリーで生まれた時，父William FitzWilliamは49歳，母Isabel Maud Deincourtは27歳であった。彼女はSir Henry de Pierrepontとの間に少なくとも2人の息子をもうけた。彼女は1348年以後に故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Fitzwilliam(이)가 1302년 영국 요르크실르 리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William FitzWilliam(은)는 49세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabel Maud Deincourt(은)는 27세였다. 그녀는 Sir Henry de Pierrepont(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1348년 이후 그녀의 고향에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Fitzwilliam nasceu em 1302, em Emley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, William FitzWilliam, tinha 49 anos e sua mãe, Isabel Maud Deincourt, tinha 27 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 2 filhos com Sir Henry de Pierrepont. Ela faleceu após 1348, em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Fitzwilliam родилась в 1302 г. в местности эмли, еркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени William FitzWilliam было 49 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabel Maud Deincourt, 27. У неё было по крайней мере 2 сына от Sir Henry de Pierrepont. Она умерла после 1348 г. в её родном городе.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret Fitzwilliam nació en 1302, en Emley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, William FitzWilliam, tenía 49 años y su mamá, Isabel Maud Deincourt, tenía 27 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos con Sir Henry de Pierrepont. Murió después de 1348, en su pueblo.
